claim: Norway's stable base shape is 4-2-3-1 with frequent 4-4-2 or 4-4-1-1 defensive phases.
why_challengeable: Player-specific role changes can alter the effective shape even when the lineup card looks similar.
required_checks: review kickoff and defensive phase shapes across the main 2025 competitive sample
separate Sorloth-wide starts from Bobb-wide starts
invalidate_if: video review shows the defensive first line is usually not a two-forward line

claim: Odegaard is Norway's key open-play organizer and primary set-piece conductor.
why_challengeable: This should be tested against matches where he is absent or limited.
required_checks: review chance creation and final-ball patterns in matches without a full Odegaard role
check whether Aasgaard, Thorstvedt, or Bobb has taken over that role in any high-value sample
invalidate_if: competitive samples show Norway can reliably break organized blocks and maintain set-piece quality without Odegaard

claim: Nusa is Norway's main left-side carrier and transition accelerator.
why_challengeable: A few standout matches can overstate how consistently that role appears.
required_checks: compare his role against strong, mid-level, and weak opponents
check how Norway progresses when he does not start
invalidate_if: larger samples show Norway mainly progresses through the right side or central direct play instead

claim: Sorloth gives Norway a second route through aerial play, box occupation, and narrow two-forward behavior.
why_challengeable: His impact can differ sharply between central starts and wide-narrow starts.
required_checks: separate central-9 samples from narrow-wide samples
review touch map, duel map, and shot map in both roles
invalidate_if: role review shows his wide starts do not materially improve dual-target box pressure

claim: Bobb is best described as a right-side variation piece rather than Norway's most stable locked-in starter.
why_challengeable: Health, minute-management, and recency make this role unstable.
required_checks: check his 2026 pre-World-Cup start rate and minute load
review whether Solbakken treats him as a default starter or matchup tool
invalidate_if: newer samples show Bobb has become the stable first-choice right-sided starter in high-value matches

claim: Norway's main defensive risk comes from space behind the fullbacks, recovery defending, and lateral midfield coverage after aggressive attacking phases.
why_challengeable: Official match pages confirm lineups and results but do not fully prove the exact defensive failure mode.
required_checks: add video review or detailed tactical reports
separate structural risk from one-off match states
invalidate_if: deeper event or video review shows the main defensive issue is elsewhere

claim: When leading, Solbakken tends to stabilize the game without fully removing the transition outlet.
why_challengeable: This needs more explicit leading-state substitution and shape evidence.
required_checks: review first and second substitution waves in clear leading states
check whether a speed or target outlet stays on the pitch
invalidate_if: larger samples show Norway quickly drops into a much lower and more passive shell once ahead
